tools.name: request_access
tools.description: Use when someone wants to find work/jobs (candidate) or hire/find candidates (employer) but the authenticated role tools are not usable yet. Explains role-specific onboarding (candidate profile interview, employer key generation), MCP key location, and next steps for both surfaces.

tools.name: candidate.search_jobs
tools.description: Semantically rank published jobs against the authenticated candidate profile (embedding-based fit). Optional tier (best|good|weak) narrows to a match-quality band — start with tier:'best' for the strongest fits and cascade only if needed; omit for the full ranked list (read tierCounts for the band s

tools.name: candidate.get_job
tools.description: Fetch a single published job by id.

tools.name: candidate.get_applications
tools.description: List the candidate's own job applications (newest first) with status, dates, the originating interview score, and a job summary. Returns only the caller's applications — no jobId input, so it is not an enumeration surface.

tools.name: candidate.set_saved_job
tools.description: Set whether a PUBLISHED job is in the candidate's saved list (idempotent desired-state, NOT a toggle — pass saved:true to bookmark, saved:false to remove). Returns NOT_FOUND for a missing or non-public job.

tools.name: candidate.get_saved_jobs
tools.description: List the candidate's saved (bookmarked) jobs, newest first. Only currently PUBLISHED jobs are returned — a job saved earlier then closed/archived is omitted.

tools.name: candidate.accept_invitation
tools.description: Accept an employer's invitation to a job (INVITED -> APPLIED). Idempotent — accepting an already-accepted invite succeeds. Returns NOT_INVITED when there is no open invitation (e.g. already withdrawn/declined), and NOT_FOUND when the job/invite is not found or the job is no longer public.

tools.name: candidate.decline_invitation
tools.description: Decline an employer's invitation to a job (INVITED -> DECLINED). TERMINAL — a declined invite blocks any re-invite from the employer, so only decline when the candidate is sure. Idempotent (declining again succeeds). Returns NOT_INVITED when there is no open invitation, NOT_FOUND when the job/invite

tools.name: candidate.withdraw_application
tools.description: Withdraw the candidate's active application to a job (APPLIED -> WITHDRAWN). Idempotent (withdrawing again succeeds). Returns NOT_APPLIED when there is no active application (e.g. only an open invitation, or already declined), and NOT_FOUND when no application exists for the job.

tools.name: candidate.apply_to_job
tools.description: Apply the candidate to a PUBLISHED job, reusing their evaluated profile interview as evidence. Idempotent (re-applying succeeds; `reused` is true when an application row already existed). Requires a completed + evaluated interview — otherwise returns GATE_LOCKED / GATE_EVALUATING / GATE_FAILED. A mi

tools.name: employer.list_jobs
tools.description: List jobs owned by the current employer account.

tools.name: employer.get_job
tools.description: Fetch a single employer job record by id. Returns NOT_FOUND for missing jobs and for jobs owned by another employer (no existence leak).

tools.name: employer.create_job
tools.description: Parse raw text via Gemini AI and create a DRAFT job under the current employer. Synchronous: latency 5-30s. The agent can then call employer.update_job to refine fields and employer.publish_job to make it live.

tools.name: employer.update_job
tools.description: Update the fields of an existing employer job. Accepts a partial whitelist; rawInput and dataSource are not editable (the wrapper auto-flips dataSource to USER_EDITED on every agent update). Non-owner reads return NOT_FOUND.

tools.name: employer.publish_job
tools.description: Transition a DRAFT job to PUBLISHED. Returns NOT_FOUND for missing or non-owner jobs and CONFLICT when the job is not in DRAFT.

tools.name: employer.close_job
tools.description: Transition a PUBLISHED job to CLOSED. Returns NOT_FOUND for missing or non-owner jobs and CONFLICT when the job is not in PUBLISHED.

tools.name: employer.archive_job
tools.description: Transition a CLOSED job to ARCHIVED. Returns NOT_FOUND for missing or non-owner jobs and CONFLICT when the job is not in CLOSED.

tools.name: employer.delete_job
tools.description: Permanently delete a DRAFT job that was never published. Returns NOT_FOUND for missing or non-owner jobs and CONFLICT when the job is not in DRAFT.

tools.name: employer.search_candidates_for_job
tools.description: Semantically rank discoverable (interviewed) candidates against one of the employer's own jobs, with a per-candidate fit score AND a white-box explanation. WORKFLOW for finding the best hire: 1) call with tier:'best' to get the strongest candidates (cover the required skills + proven in interview),

tools.name: employer.search_candidates_by_query
tools.description: Free-form semantic search across discoverable (interviewed) candidates with no job context. The query is embedded and candidates are ranked by semantic similarity — a preliminary search with no per-vacancy fit score (there is no vacancy to fit). For a scored ranking, use employer.search_candidates_f

tools.name: employer.get_candidate
tools.description: Fetch a discoverable candidate by user id. Returns search-entry shape plus a light interview slice (overallScore + summary + completedAt + evaluatedAt) and `existingApplications`: every JobApplication this candidate has on any of the employer's jobs (all 4 statuses, all 4 job statuses) so the agent

tools.name: employer.get_candidate_evidence
tools.description: Fetch the interview EVIDENCE (facts proven in the interview + their Q&A, the interview summary, the résumé summary, and GitHub/LinkedIn signals) for ONE candidate AGAINST one of your published jobs — the white-box basis to explain WHY a candidate ranks where they do. Use it AFTER search_candidates_f

tools.name: employer.invite_candidate
tools.description: Invite a discoverable candidate to one of the employer's PUBLISHED jobs. Creates a JobApplication with status=INVITED. If a prior WITHDRAWN row exists for this (candidate, job) pair, the row is UPDATEd back to INVITED (re-invite is allowed after the candidate withdrew on their own). INVITED, APPLIED

tools.name: employer.list_applicants
tools.description: List the live (APPLIED) applicants on one of the employer's jobs. The candidate showcase + interview overallScore/summary are always returned; contact fields are only included when the application is SHORTLISTED or HIRED.

tools.name: employer.set_review_status
tools.description: Update the employer review state on an application (NEW / REVIEWING / SHORTLISTED / REJECTED / HIRED). SHORTLISTED and HIRED unlock the candidate's direct contact in subsequent list_applicants / get_applicant_detail calls. WITHDRAWN applications surface as CONFLICT.

tools.name: employer.get_applicant_detail
tools.description: Full applicant bundle: resume, interview light slice (overallScore + summary + facts), GitHub analysis, LinkedIn analysis. The verbatim transcript is delivered by employer.get_applicant_transcript; the resume's contact fields are blanked unless the application is SHORTLISTED or HIRED.

tools.name: employer.get_applicant_transcript
tools.description: Verbatim interview transcript for one applicant. Ownership-only gate (same as the UI Download button — no SHORTLISTED/HIRED requirement). Returns an empty array when the source interview never produced turns.

tools.name: employer.list_invitations
tools.description: List the pending (INVITED) candidates on one of the employer's jobs — candidates who have been invited but have not yet accepted or declined.

tools.name: employer.cancel_invitation
tools.description: Cancel a pending invitation sent to a candidate. The invitation record is deleted; the employer can re-invite the same candidate later.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "employer.invite_candidate" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Invite a discoverable candidate to one of the employer's PUBLISHED jobs. Creates a JobApplication with status=INVITED. If a prior WITHDRAWN row exists for this …

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: employer.search_candidates_for_job, employer.get_candidate, employer.get_candidate_evidence, employer.get_applicant_detail, employer.get_applicant_transcript · Private data: employer.get_job, employer.list_applicants, employer.set_review_status, employer.get_applicant_detail, employer.cancel_invitation · Public sink: employer.publish_job · Destructive: candidate.set_saved_job, employer.delete_job.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: employer.search_candidates_for_job, employer.get_candidate, employer.get_candidate_evidence, employer.get_applicant_detail, employer.get_applicant_transcript · Private data: employer.get_job, employer.list_applicants, employer.set_review_status, employer.get_applicant_detail, employer.cancel_invitation · Public sink: employer.publish_job · Destructive: candidate.set_saved_job, employer.delete_job

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
